The [[Thrugii Asteroid Belt]] was a widespread [[Asteroid field/Legends|belt]] of [[Asteroid/Legends|orbiting material]] in the [[Socorro system/Legends|Socorro system]]. It was present in three different orbitals, and astrogation reports suggested that they were the remains of three [[Planet/Legends|planets]], destroyed when the system's primary, [[Sokor/Legends|Sokor]], blossomed into a [[Red giant/Legends|red giant]].

The [[Kanauer Corporation/Legends|Kanauer Corporation]] claimed all rights to the Belt and mined the asteroids for small quantities of [[Doonium/Legends|doonium]] and [[turminum]]. Since the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had no presence in the Socorro system, the corporation felt free to turn outsiders away at [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] point.

==Behind the scenes==
[[Patricia A. Jackson]]'s short story "[[When the Domino Falls]]," published in [[August]] [[1994]] in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 3|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 3]], introduced the planet [[Thrugii]], located in an asteroid belt that was at least one hyperspace jump away from [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]].<ref name="Domino">{{JournalCite|3|When the Domino Falls|reprint=1}}</ref> However, the [[1997]] supplement ''[[The Black Sands of Socorro]]'', also by Jackson, instead introduced the Thrugii Asteroid Belt in the Socorro system, and established that Socorro and [[Neftali/Legends|Neftali]] were the only planets in the system.<ref name="BSS" /> The [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion]] later established the [[Thrugii system/Legends|Thrugii system]] in the same sector as Socorro.<ref name="EAOC" />
